Title: The SlGRAS9-SlMYC1 regulatory module controls glandular trichome formation and modulates resilience to pest in tomato

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: type VI glandular trichomes in leaves, sepals, petals, and fruits
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: defense against herbivorous pests including spider mites and aphids
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: high in type VI glandular trichomes and trichome-rich tissues, highest in sepals; lower in trichome-rich accessions and higher in trichome-poor accessions
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: high in trichomes from leaves, stems, fruits, and sepals
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: directly represses SlMYC1 transcription by binding its promoter; indirectly reduces TPS gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: No validated interaction with Wo; Hair; SlCycB2; SlMYC1 in firefly luciferase complementation assays
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Wo; Hair; SlCycB2; SlMYC1 (tested, no interaction detected)
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; scanning electron microscopy trichome counts; GC-MS metabolite profiling; RT-qPCR; dual-luciferase assay; EMSA; yeast one-hybrid; genetic interaction with Slmyc1-CR double mutant; pest bioassays
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: indirect positive/negative control of terpenoid biosynthesis through regulation of type VI glandular trichome formation and repression of SlMYC1-dependent TPS expression
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: spider mite infestation; aphid infestation; greenhouse growth conditions
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: transcriptomic profiling from prior published dataset; DAP-seq evidence from prior study; no accession reported in this paper
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: loss of SlGRAS9 increases type VI glandular trichome density and elevates volatile terpenoid accumulation in leaves, sepals, petals, and fruits, enhancing pest tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: acts as an upstream negative regulator of the SlMYC1-controlled module governing type VI glandular trichome formation and terpenoid accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: slight reduction of long trichomes (types I/II/III) in fruits and sepals of knockout plants; no other penalty reported
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: editing or down-regulating SlGRAS9 is a promising strategy to increase glandular trichomes, terpenoid-based defense, and pest resilience in tomato
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlGRAS9 is highly expressed in glandular trichomes and negatively correlated with trichome-rich accessions. 2. CRISPR knockout increased type VI glandular trichomes and volatile terpenoids across leaves, sepals, petals, and fruits. 3. SlGRAS9 directly binds the SlMYC1 promoter and represses its transcription. 4. Double-mutant analysis indicates SlGRAS9 acts upstream of SlMYC1. 5. SlGRAS9 knockout improves tolerance to spider mites and aphids.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Tomato homolog of Arabidopsis SCL8. SlGRAS9 did not directly regulate tested TPS promoters in dual-luciferase assays, supporting indirect control via SlMYC1.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: type VI glandular trichomes in leaves, sepals, petals, and fruits
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: defense against herbivorous pests including spider mites and aphids
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: higher in high-trichome-density accessions; increased in young leaves and fruit trichomes of Slgras9-CR plants
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: expressed in trichome-rich tissues including leaves and fruit trichomes
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: TPS genes including SlTPS1; SlTPS5; SlTPS12; and previously reported SlTPS9; SlTPS20
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: positively regulates TPS gene expression, at least partly indirectly; previously shown to activate SlTPS1, SlTPS5 and SlTPS12
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Previously reported to form a regulatory module with Wo and be recruited to TPS promoters by Wo; this paper supports action downstream of SlGRAS9
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: overexpression; CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; scanning electron microscopy trichome counts; GC-MS metabolite profiling; genetic interaction with Slgras9-CR; pest bioassays; RT-qPCR
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: positive regulation of type VI glandular trichome development and associated terpenoid biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: spider mite infestation; aphid infestation; greenhouse growth conditions
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: overexpression increases type VI glandular trichomes and terpenoid accumulation, whereas knockout reduces type VI glandular trichomes and terpenoids to near-undetectable levels
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: essential positive regulator of type VI glandular trichome differentiation and a major activator of terpenoid accumulation in multiple tomato tissues
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: knockout shifts glandular trichome composition toward type VII and VII-like trichomes and strongly reduces pest tolerance; no overexpression penalty reported
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: overexpressing or up-regulating SlMYC1 is a promising route to enhance terpenoid-mediated pest resistance in tomato
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlMYC1 overexpression increased type VI glandular trichome density in leaves, sepals, petals, and fruits. 2. SlMYC1 knockout decreased type VI trichomes while increasing type VII/VII-like trichomes. 3. Overexpression elevated terpenoid accumulation, whereas knockout reduced terpenoids to almost undetectable levels. 4. Slmyc1 mutation was epistatic to Slgras9, indicating SlMYC1 acts downstream of SlGRAS9. 5. SlMYC1 overexpression improved tolerance to spider mites and aphids.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This paper positions SlMYC1 as the downstream effector of SlGRAS9. Direct binding to TPS promoters was not established here; prior work indicates indirect activation through Wo.
